title: Global Health Officials Release Emergency Funds to Fight Ebola Outbreak
subtitle: As cases rise in the DRC and Uganda, international experts work to stop the spread of a dangerous virus.

summary.en: The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has announced $107 million in emergency funding to fight an Ebola outbreak. This outbreak is currently affecting the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda. Health officials report that the number of confirmed cases is approaching 1,000. While the risk to the general global population remains low, experts are working hard to contain the virus locally. Challenges such as a lack of equipment and public mistrust continue to make the response difficult.
summary.zh: 美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）宣布提供一億零七百萬美元的緊急資金，以對抗伊波拉病毒疫情。這場疫情目前正影響剛果民主共和國與烏干達。衛生官員報告確診病例數正接近一千例。儘管對全球大眾的風險仍然很低，專家們正努力在當地控制病毒。設備短缺與公眾不信任等挑戰，持續使防疫工作面臨困難。

paragraphs.en: Health officials have announced a major new effort to stop the spread of a serious virus in Africa. On Thursday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) announced that it will provide $107 million in emergency funding to help the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda manage an ongoing Ebola outbreak. This new support adds to the nearly $910 million that international donors have already promised to help fight the disease.
paragraphs.zh: 衛生官員宣布了一項重大的新計畫，旨在阻止一種嚴重病毒在非洲的傳播。美國疾病管制與預防中心（CDC）於週四宣布，將提供一億零七百萬美元的緊急資金，協助剛果民主共和國（DRC）與烏干達控制當前的伊波拉病毒疫情。這筆新援助將挹注於國際捐助者先前已承諾的近九億一千萬美元資金，共同對抗該疾病。

paragraphs.en: According to The Guardian, this outbreak is now the third largest in history. Dr. Satish K. Pillai, an incident manager for the CDC, explained that the number of confirmed cases is nearing 1,000 across 31 health zones in the DRC. In Uganda, there are currently 31 confirmed cases. As of June 15, the CDC reported 837 confirmed cases in the DRC and 19 in Uganda, with a total of 198 deaths across both nations.
paragraphs.zh: 根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，此次疫情已成為史上第三大規模。CDC 的事件經理薩提什·皮萊（Satish K. Pillai）博士說明，剛果民主共和國境內三十一個衛生區的確診病例數已接近一千例。烏干達目前則有三十一例確診。截至六月十五日，CDC 通報剛果民主共和國有八百三十七例確診，烏干達有十九例，兩國合計共一百九十八人死亡。

paragraphs.en: The virus involved in this outbreak is a rare type of Ebola known as Bundibugyo viral disease (BVD). This is a type of hemorrhagic fever, which is a serious illness that can cause internal bleeding. In recent outbreaks, this specific virus has been fatal for 30% to 50% of those who become infected. Because of this, African health leaders are worried. They warn that if the current rate of infection continues, the outbreak could become the worst one ever recorded and might take an entire year to bring under control.
paragraphs.zh: 此次疫情涉及的病毒是一種罕見的伊波拉病毒，稱為邦迪布焦病毒疾病（BVD）。這是一種出血熱，屬於可能導致體內出血的嚴重疾病。在近期的疫情中，感染此特定病毒的致死率介於百分之三十至百分之五十之間。正因如此，非洲的衛生領袖感到憂心。他們警告，若目前的感染速度持續下去，這場疫情可能成為有史以來最嚴重的一次，且可能需要整整一年才能獲得控制。

paragraphs.en: Despite these concerns, officials emphasize that the global risk remains low. The virus is spread through direct contact with the blood or body fluids of an infected person, or by touching surfaces that have been contaminated. This is very different from diseases like Covid-19 or measles, which can spread through the air and are much easier to catch. Even so, the US and 21 other countries have put travel restrictions in place for people coming from the affected areas. Some experts have criticized these rules, saying they make it harder for medical teams to get the help they need to the right places.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管存在這些擔憂，官員強調全球風險仍然很低。該病毒是透過直接接觸感染者的血液或體液，或是觸摸受污染的表面來傳播。這與新冠肺炎或麻疹等可透過空氣傳播、且更容易感染的疾病截然不同。即便如此，美國與其他二十一個國家仍針對來自受影響地區的人員實施了旅遊限制。部分專家批評這些規定，認為這使得醫療團隊更難將所需的援助送達正確的地點。

paragraphs.en: There are several reasons why stopping this outbreak has been difficult. The Guardian reports that local health authorities are struggling with a shortage of personal protective equipment and vehicles needed to safely move the bodies of those who have died. Additionally, a lack of trust in the community has made it harder for medical workers to do their jobs. To help, the CDC has sent 23 field staff to the region to help with disease investigations, and they have 125 staff members working across the two countries.
paragraphs.zh: 遏止此次疫情困難重重，原因有幾點。《衛報》報導指出，當地衛生當局正苦於缺乏個人防護裝備，以及安全運送遺體所需的車輛。此外，社區民眾缺乏信任感，也讓醫護人員的工作更加艱難。為了提供協助，CDC 已派遣二十三名現場工作人員前往該地區協助進行疾病調查，目前在兩國境內共有一百二十五名工作人員在執行任務。

paragraphs.en: This situation is happening at a time when the world is also focused on the Fifa World Cup, which is being hosted by the US, Canada, and Mexico. Because the tournament brings many visitors from all over the world, the CDC is staying alert. Dr. Pillai noted that CDC staff hold meetings twice a week with officials in the US cities hosting the World Cup. So far, however, the health issues seen in those cities have been common problems related to large crowds, such as heat-related illnesses, rather than anything related to the Ebola outbreak.
paragraphs.zh: 此情況發生的同時，全球焦點也正集中在由美國、加拿大與墨西哥共同主辦的國際足總世界盃（Fifa World Cup）。由於賽事吸引了來自世界各地的遊客，CDC 保持高度警覺。皮萊博士指出，CDC 工作人員每週會與主辦世界盃的美國城市官員召開兩次會議。然而到目前為止，這些城市出現的健康問題多為大型群聚活動常見的狀況，例如與高溫相關的疾病，而非與伊波拉疫情有關的案例。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the focus remains on controlling the virus at its source. African health leaders have pointed out that while many countries have promised money, less than 10% of the pledged funds have actually been received. Without more support, the effort to contain the virus will continue to face major obstacles. For now, the CDC says its main goal is to stop the spread in the DRC and Uganda while ensuring that the US is ready to respond if any cases appear domestically.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，重點仍放在從源頭控制病毒。非洲衛生領袖指出，雖然許多國家承諾提供資金，但實際收到的款項不到承諾金額的百分之十。若無更多支持，遏止病毒的努力將持續面臨重大障礙。目前，CDC 表示其主要目標是阻止病毒在剛果民主共和國與烏干達擴散，同時確保美國已做好準備，以應對國內可能出現的任何病例。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To increase the total amount of something that already exists.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 增加、增添（某事物的總量）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指在原有的金額上再增加。文中：This new support adds to the nearly $910 million that international donors have already promised to help fight the disease.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To successfully manage or stop a difficult situation or disease.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於疫情或火災等負面情況。文中：and might take an entire year to bring under control.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To establish or implement a rule, system, or plan.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指實施旅遊限制。文中：the US and 21 other countries have put travel restrictions in place for people coming from the affected areas.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Remaining watchful and ready to react to potential problems.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指 CDC 對疫情保持高度關注。文中：Because the tournament brings many visitors from all over the world, the CDC is staying alert.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda

previousArticles.title: Global Response Grows as Ebola Outbreak Spreads in the DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is facing a serious Ebola outbreak that has already caused hundreds of deaths. International leaders at the G7 summit have pledged significant financial support to help contain the virus. The current outbreak involves a rare strain that is difficult to treat with existing medical tools. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the health risks and the economic impact of the disease. Experts warn that global cooperation is essential to manage this public health emergency effectively.

previousArticles.title: International Support Grows as Ebola Outbreak Challenges DRC
previousArticles.summaryEn: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is currently managing a serious Ebola outbreak involving a rare viral strain. International groups, including the EU and G7, have promised nearly 500 million euros to help with medical support and research. Local communities in Bunia are struggling with both the fear of infection and a sharp decline in their daily income. Experts note that the virus is difficult to treat because existing vaccines are not fully effective against this specific strain. Global health authorities continue to emphasize that international cooperation is essential to stop the spread of the disease.

quiz.question: According to the report as of June 15, what was the total number of confirmed Ebola cases in Uganda?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，文中提到在剛果民主共和國（DRC）有 837 例，而在烏干達（Uganda）則有 19 例。選項 A 的 31 是指烏干達「目前」的病例數，而非 6 月 15 日的統計數據。選項 C 是指剛果民主共和國跨越 31 個衛生區的病例接近數，選項 D 則是剛果民主共和國在 6 月 15 日的確診數，皆非烏干達的正確數據。

quiz.question: Why might experts criticize the travel restrictions imposed by the US and other countries?

options.A: Because the restrictions are ineffective against airborne diseases like Covid-19.
options.B: Because they believe the Ebola virus will inevitably spread to the US during the World Cup.
options.C: Because these measures create logistical barriers for medical personnel trying to reach affected areas.
options.D: Because the restrictions are too expensive for the CDC to maintain during the current outbreak.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第四段，專家批評旅行限制是因為這些規定使得醫療團隊難以將必要的協助送達正確的地點。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章提到 Ebola 與 Covid-19 傳播方式不同；選項 B 並非專家批評的原因，文章指出專家是擔心物流受阻；選項 D 則未在文中提及，屬於過度推論。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'pledged' most closely mean in the context of 'less than 10% of the pledged funds have actually been received'?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在文章語境中，pledged 指的是國際捐助者先前「承諾」給予的資金，但實際收到的金額卻不到 10%。選項 B（賺取）與選項 C（借貸）皆不符合語境。選項 D（捐贈）雖然與金錢有關，但 pledged 強調的是一種「承諾」或「保證」的狀態，而非實際已完成的捐贈行為，故 A 為最精確的同義詞。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article regarding the current Ebola outbreak?

options.A: The World Cup is the biggest risk factor for the global spread of the Ebola virus.
options.B: The Ebola outbreak in Africa is severe and faces significant challenges, requiring more than just financial promises.
options.C: The CDC has successfully contained the virus in the DRC and Uganda through its recent funding.
options.D: The Bundibugyo viral disease is less dangerous than previously thought because it is not airborne.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。本文旨在探討 Ebola 疫情的嚴重性、面臨的資源短缺與信任問題，並強調僅有資金承諾是不夠的，還需要實質的行動與支持。選項 A 過度誇大了世界盃的影響；選項 C 與文章內容矛盾，因為疫情仍在持續且面臨障礙；選項 D 雖然提到 Ebola 不透過空氣傳播，但文章並未因此將其定義為「較不危險」，該病毒仍有 30% 至 50% 的致死率。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
